subject: English
title: We’re Not Afraid to Die... if We Can All Be Together

questions.text: Who is the author of 'We’re Not Afraid to Die... if We Can All Be Together'?
questions.explanation: Gordon Cook is the captain of the ship and narrator of the story.

questions.text: What was the name of the ship in the story?
questions.explanation: Wavewalker was a 23-meter, 30-ton wooden-hulled beauty.

questions.text: From where did the narrator start his journey?
questions.explanation: The journey began in July 1976 from Plymouth.

questions.text: Who did the narrator want to duplicate the voyage of?
questions.explanation: He wanted to repeat the round-the-world voyage made 200 years earlier.

questions.text: Who were the crewmen the narrator took with him from Cape Town?
questions.explanation: American Larry Vigil and Swiss Herb Seigler were hired to help cross the Southern Indian Ocean.

questions.text: The first leg of the planned ______ voyage passed pleasantly.
questions.explanation: The first three years and 105,000 kilometers went smoothly.

questions.text: On which day did the weather begin to turn bad?
questions.explanation: At dawn on January 2, the waves became gigantic.

questions.text: What did the crew do to slow down the boat during the storm?
questions.explanation: They dropped the storm jib and lashed a heavy mooring rope in a loop across the stern.

questions.text: What hit the ship that the narrator initially thought was a cloud?
questions.explanation: A tremendous explosion shook the deck as a vertical wave hit the ship.

questions.text: The narrator's head smashed into the ______ when the wave hit.
questions.explanation: He was thrown overboard and then hit back into the boat, smashing his head.

questions.text: What was the name of the narrator's daughter?
questions.explanation: Sue (Suzanne) was seven years old during the voyage.

questions.text: What was the name of the narrator's son?
questions.explanation: Jonathan was six years old.

questions.text: Who said, 'We are sinking!'?
questions.explanation: The narrator's wife, Mary, appeared when the decks were smashed.

questions.text: How did Larry and Herb act during the crisis?
questions.explanation: The crewmen worked tirelessly to pump water out of the ship.

questions.text: What happened to the electric pump?
questions.explanation: The electric pump short-circuited after the handpumps started blocking with debris.

questions.text: After 36 hours of continuous pumping, they finally ______.
options: Reached the last few centimeters of water
questions.explanation: By January 4, they had almost cleared the water.

questions.text: What did they eat for their first meal in almost two days?
questions.explanation: Mary found some corned beef and biscuits for them to eat.

questions.text: When did the storm start again after a brief respite?
questions.explanation: By 4 p.m. on Jan 4, black clouds began building up again.

questions.text: Who said the title of the story?
questions.explanation: The six-year-old Jonathan showed immense bravery by saying he wasn't afraid to die.

questions.text: What was the destination the narrator was aiming for?
questions.explanation: They were looking for a tiny island in the vast ocean called Ile Amsterdam.

questions.text: What did Sue give the narrator to cheer him up?
questions.explanation: She made a card with 'funny people' to make them laugh.

questions.text: What was Ile Amsterdam described as?
questions.explanation: It was a bleak piece of volcanic rock with little vegetation.

questions.text: How many inhabitants were on the island?
questions.explanation: 28 inhabitants cheered them as they reached the island.

questions.text: What happened to Sue's head injury?
questions.explanation: She had a recurring bump that needed several operations to remove blood clots.

questions.text: What was the Southern Indian Ocean described as in the text?
questions.explanation: The Southern Indian Ocean is known for its dangerous and rough conditions.

questions.text: The narrator spent ______ years preparing for the voyage.
questions.explanation: He and his wife had spent 16 years honing their seafaring skills.

questions.text: The children's room was located ______.
questions.explanation: They were in their bunks when the narrator went to check on them.

questions.text: When the wave hit, the narrator felt he was ______.
questions.explanation: He felt very peaceful while he thought he was dying.

questions.text: The island was used as a ______ base.
questions.explanation: Ile Amsterdam is a French scientific research station.

questions.text: What kind of boat was Wavewalker?
questions.explanation: It was specifically built and tested for rough weather.

questions.text: Who said the narrator was the 'best daddy in the whole world'?
questions.explanation: Jonathan said this when they finally found the island.

questions.text: What did the narrator use to calculate their position after the equipment failed?
options: A spare compass and a sextant
questions.explanation: He made calculations using a compass not adjusted for magnetic variation.

questions.text: The waves on January 2 were as high as ______.
questions.explanation: The waves were 15 meters high, as high as the main mast.

questions.text: The narrator found a ______ electric pump under the chartroom floor.
questions.explanation: This spare pump helped them when the others failed.

questions.text: How did the family spend Christmas?
options: On the boat with a Christmas tree
questions.explanation: They celebrated 3,500 kilometers east of Cape Town on the boat.

questions.text: What was the age of the narrator's daughter?
questions.explanation: Sue was seven years old.

questions.text: The narrator's left ribs were ______ and his mouth filled with blood.
questions.explanation: He suffered serious injuries but kept working to save the ship.

questions.text: What did they use to cover the gaping holes in the deck?
questions.explanation: He stretched canvas to stop water from streaming below.

questions.text: The voyage was meant to cover ______ in three years.
questions.explanation: The first leg was 105,000 km.

questions.text: What was the main theme of the story?
questions.explanation: It highlights how courage and unity can help overcome extreme challenges.
